marketState: neutral — exceptional current demand offset by bearish 4-week seasonality and elevated storage
summary: Front-month NG=F ($3.313) sits slightly above the fair-value midpoint ($2.958) but well within the $2.458–$3.458 band, rendering price neither stretched nor cheap. The dominant near-term feature is an extraordinary demand spike: current gas demand stands at 169.8 (+36% above 5-year normal) and is at the very top of the 7-day range with a 24-hour surge of +25.6, signalling an intense heat event in progress. Against this, seasonality is a clear headwind — historical data shows only a 19% probability of prices being higher in 4 weeks with a typical 4-week move of −1.8%, though reliability is low (10–90% spread of 51 pp this week). Storage at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average remains a structural bear, while managed-money positioning at the 17th 5-year percentile (net −84,909 contracts) sits at historically short levels — a contrarian long signal awaiting a catalyst. Forward weather demand (1–7D and 8–16D) and forward fair-value estimates (plus7D through plus21D) are unavailable, materially raising uncertainty across both trade horizons.

longTerm.rationale: Bearish seasonal odds (19% probability higher in 4 weeks, typical −1.8% 4-week move) combined with storage running +153 Bcf above the 5-year average and daily/weekly death crosses (50<200 EMA) argue against initiating a directional position. While managed-money positioning at the 17th percentile provides a contrarian long argument, no short-squeeze catalyst has emerged and the winter curve (Jan '27 at $4.47) shows the market already prices seasonal demand, capping front-month upside. Missing forward weather and forward fair-value data lower conviction further.
longTerm.trigger: Enter long if price pulls back to the $3.00–3.10 zone (near fair-value midpoint) AND weekly storage shows a negative surprise (injection below the 5-year seasonal norm) OR if demand sustains at least 15% above 5-year normal for two consecutive weeks with confirmed forward-weather support

shortTerm.rationale: The 24-hour demand surge to 169.8 (+36% vs 5y, +25.6 in 24h, top of 7-day range) is the single strongest near-term data point and signals an intense heat event capable of short-term price spikes. The 4-hour timeframe is bullish with a positive MACD and a 50>200 EMA cross, and the top of the fair-value band ($3.458) offers a natural first profit target. Conviction is capped at low because forward weather data (1–7D, 8–16D) is unavailable and the weekly trend is bearish — position size is halved to 0.5R accordingly.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $3.18 (stop level) would invalidate; a weekly storage injection materially above the 5-year seasonal average would also negate the thesis.

keyDrivers: Demand at 169.8, +36% vs 5y normal — top of 7-day range with a +25.6 spike in 24h
Bearish seasonality: only 19% probability of higher price in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move −1.8%
COT managed-money net at 17th 5-year percentile — historically short, contrarian bullish signal
Storage surplus: +153 Bcf vs 5-year average with ongoing injections, structural bearish weight
Winter curve steep contango (Jan '27 at $4.47 vs Aug '26 at $3.31), limiting front-month upside
risks: Forward weather demand (1–7D and 8–16D) unavailable — heat event may be short-lived, quickly fading the near-term long thesis
Elevated storage surplus (+153 Bcf) could accelerate if demand moderates, adding downward pressure to front-month prices
Daily and weekly death cross (50<200 EMA) and bearish weekly trend signal structural downside risk if seasonal norms reassert
